title: Gmail OAuth redirect URI mismatch error
symptom: When attempting to authenticate with Gmail OAuth, the user receives an 'Error 400: redirect_uri_mismatch' message. This occurs due to the redirect URL being incorrectly set to 'localhost'.
not_this_if: Redirect URI is correctly set in Google Cloud Console
root_cause: The redirect URI mismatch error occurs because the redirect URL configured in the Google Cloud Console does not match the one being used by n8n. If the `.env` file is not properly loaded in the Docker environment, it may default to 'localhost', causing this mismatch.
fix_steps: Open the docker-compose.yml file for your n8n setup.
Add the `-env_file` directive to ensure the `.env` file is loaded.
Restart the Docker container to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Ensure the `.env` file is loaded in your Docker setup by modifying the docker-compose.yml file.
